skill.name: youtube-channel-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users automatically extract structured channel data from YouTube search results via BrowserAct API. Agent should proactively apply this skill when users express needs like finding YouTube channels about specific topics, collecting data on YouTube content creators, tracking YouTube influencers in specific industries, getting YouTube channel information for competitor analysis, searching for YouTube channels related to keywords, monitoring YouTube channel updates for specific keywords, finding YouTube channels that recently published videos, extracting YouTube channel subscriber counts, discovering YouTube vloggers in specific niches, building a YouTube channel database for market research, batch extracting YouTube channel links and descriptions, or monitoring competitor channel growth.
skill.summary: Automatically extract structured YouTube channel data including subscriber counts, handles, and descriptions using the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 51 static findings across 2 files (189 lines). All network findings are false positives - HTTP requests target the documented BrowserAct API (api.browseract.com) for legitimate channel data extraction. All 11 env_access findings are expected behavior for reading the required BROWSERACT_API_KEY. The 21 external_commands flags are false positives - the static analyzer misidentified markdown code fence indicators as Ruby backtick execution. All 6 weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives as no cryptographic code exists in the skill. One critical heuristic finding (code execution + network + credential combination) is a false positive - this combination is necessary and expected for a legitimate API client skill. Risk level is LOW - the skill is a documented API client with a third-party service dependency.

low_findings.title: Third-Party API Data Routing
low_findings.description: All YouTube search queries and the BROWSERACT_API_KEY are routed through the BrowserAct API service (api.browseract.com), a third-party intermediary. User search terms and API credentials pass through an external service not operated by YouTube or the AI platform.

low_findings.title: API Key Transmitted in Every Request
low_findings.description: The BROWSERACT_API_KEY is sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of every HTTP request to the BrowserAct API, including task start, status polling, and result retrieval calls.

content.user_title: Extract YouTube Channel Data via API
content.value_statement: Manually searching and recording YouTube channel information is slow and error-prone. This skill automatically extracts structured channel data including subscriber counts, handles, and descriptions from YouTube search results using the BrowserAct API.
actual_capabilities: Search YouTube channels by keyword with optional upload date filter
Extract channel title, handle, subscriber count, description, and verification status
Retrieve channel thumbnail URLs and channel page links
Monitor task execution with real-time status logs and automatic retry on failure
Detect invalid API credentials and guide users to obtain a valid key
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key set as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Relies on the BrowserAct third-party service which has its own availability and rate limits
Task execution can take several minutes due to automated browser operations
Cannot access YouTube data directly without going through the BrowserAct intermediary service

use_cases.title: Creator Discovery for Outreach Campaigns
use_cases.description: Marketing professionals can find YouTube channels related to specific topics to identify potential collaboration partners and influencers.

use_cases.title: Competitive Landscape Analysis
use_cases.description: Business analysts can track competitor channels by searching for brand-related keywords and monitoring channel growth and activity.

use_cases.title: Content Creator Database Building
use_cases.description: Researchers and data analysts can build structured databases of YouTube content creators in specific niches for market research and trend analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Channel Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube channels about artificial intelligence.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a simple channel search by topic keyword

prompt_templates.title: Search with Date Filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube channels about crypto trading that uploaded videos this week.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants recently active channels filtered by upload date

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Topic Batch Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for YouTube channels about vegan recipes and organic gardening. Run each search separately and combine the results into one list.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs channel data across multiple topics for comparison

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Channel Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube channels about productivity tools. For each channel, extract the subscriber count, handle, description, and verification status. Show the thumbnail URL for each channel.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants detailed channel profiles for competitor or influencer analysis

output_examples.input: Find YouTube channels about machine learning.
output_examples.output: Returns a list of YouTube channels matching the keyword with channel title, subscriber count, verification status, handle, description, and thumbnail URL for each result.

output_examples.input: Find YouTube channels about vegan recipes that uploaded this week.
output_examples.output: Returns filtered results showing only recently active YouTube channels in the vegan recipe niche, with subscriber counts, verification badges, and channel descriptions.

output_examples.input: Search for YouTube channels about digital marketing automation.
output_examples.output: Returns structured channel data including the channel name, subscriber numbers, verification status, handle, a short description of each channel, and the channel avatar URL.

best_practices: Verify the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before running the script to avoid authentication errors.
Monitor the terminal output for status logs to confirm the automated browser task is progressing normally.
Use specific and targeted keywords for more relevant and useful channel search results.
anti_patterns: Do not retry when the error contains Invalid authorization - the API key is invalid and requires the user to provide a correct one.
Do not assume the task is stuck if status logs are still appearing - browser automation tasks typically take several minutes.
Do not modify the script to bypass the API key check - the skill requires a valid key to function correctly.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a valid BrowserAct API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill.

faq.question: How long does a YouTube channel search take?
faq.answer: Results typically take several minutes because the skill uses automated browser operations through the BrowserAct API to perform the search.

faq.question: What data does this skill extract from YouTube channels?
faq.answer: It extracts channel title, channel page URL, verification status, handle, subscriber count, description, and thumbnail URL.

faq.question: Does this skill work across different YouTube regions?
faq.answer: The skill uses the BrowserAct API which claims to bypass geo-blocking and IP restrictions for YouTube searches.

faq.question: What happens if the search fails?
faq.answer: The skill automatically retries once. If the second attempt also fails, it stops and reports the error message to the user.

faq.question: Can I search for multiple keywords in one request?
faq.answer: The skill processes one keyword search at a time. For multiple keywords, run the skill separately for each keyword and combine the results.
